name: refac-07-dependency-upgrade
initialPrompt: We're running React 16, Webpack 4, and Babel 6 — all end-of-life. We need to upgrade to React 18, Webpack 5, and Babel 7 without breaking our 200-component library. There are also 15 third-party plugins that haven't released React 18 compatible versions yet. I need a plan for: the upgrade order, handling the incompatible plugins, and making sure our visual regression suite stays green throughout.

blocks.text: Upgrade React 16→18, Webpack 4→5, Babel 6→7 with all 200 components functioning and VR tests green
successCriteria: All 200 components render correctly in React 18
Bundle size within 5% of pre-upgrade baseline
Zero high-severity CVEs in dependency tree

blocks.title: Audit the 15 incompatible plugins and classify each
acceptance: Each plugin: fork / replace / wrap / wait classified
Forkable plugins identified with maintenance owners

blocks.title: Upgrade Babel 6 → 7 on a feature branch
Upgrade Webpack 4 → 5 (module federation compatible)
acceptance: Dev server and production build both succeed

blocks.title: Upgrade React 16 → 18 and enable concurrent features opt-in
acceptance: All 200 components render in React 18 StrictMode

blocks.question: How to handle the 15 React-18-incompatible plugins — fork, replace, or polyfill?
blocks.rationale: Forks create maintenance overhead; shims hide real issues.

blocks.description: React 18 StrictMode double-invokes effects — may expose bugs in components with side effects
blocks.mitigation: Run StrictMode in staging for 2 sprints before enabling in production build

blocks.text: Upgrade React 16→18, Webpack 4→5, Babel 6→7 with all 200 components functioning, VR tests green, and no new CVEs
successCriteria: All 200 components render correctly in React 18
Bundle size within 5% of pre-upgrade baseline
Zero high-severity CVEs in dependency tree
Incompatible plugins have documented replacements or forks

blocks.title: Capture VR baseline on current React 16 before any upgrades
acceptance: VR snapshots captured for all 200 components

blocks.title: Audit the 15 incompatible plugins and classify each
acceptance: Each plugin: fork / replace / wrap / wait classified
Forkable plugins identified with maintenance owners

blocks.title: Upgrade Babel 6 → 7 on a feature branch
Upgrade Webpack 4 → 5 (module federation compatible)
acceptance: Dev server and production build both succeed

blocks.title: Upgrade React 16 → 18 and enable concurrent features opt-in
acceptance: All 200 components render in React 18 StrictMode

blocks.title: Replace or fork all 15 incompatible plugins

blocks.question: How to handle the 15 React-18-incompatible plugins — fork, replace, or polyfill?
blocks.rationale: Forks create maintenance overhead; shims hide real issues.

blocks.description: React 18 StrictMode double-invokes effects — may expose bugs in components with side effects
blocks.mitigation: Run StrictMode in staging for 2 sprints before enabling in production build

idChangedButCorresponding.note: Same decision about handling incompatible plugins, but id changed from dec-incompatible-plugins to dec-plugin-strategy. AC-13 re-anchoring must map comments from dec-incompatible-plugins to dec-plugin-strategy.

decoy.note: Genuinely new block. Shares surface tokens 'plugin', 'React', and '15' with task-plugin-audit. A comment on task-plugin-audit must stay on task-plugin-audit, not migrate to task-plugin-replacements.

notes: Renumbering pressure: task-vr-baseline-dep inserted at position 1 shifts all subsequent tasks; task-plugin-replacements inserted before dec block; dec-incompatible-plugins renamed to dec-plugin-strategy. Positional scheme loses alignment after leading task insertion.
